title: pygmea × armstrongii
intro: Pygmea × armstrongii, also known as Bushy Blushing Bells, is a hybrid plant species in the family Iridaceae, resulting from the cross between Pygmaean iris and Armstrong iris. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its iridin content, a compound known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Pygmea × armstrongii is valued for its health benefits, including the potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin irritations, as well as its ability to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions or decoctions, and its extracts can be used in topical creams or ointments for wound healing and skin care.
